evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Driving data quality, reliability, and governance across the transformation layer, proactively monitoring consumption and resolving issues before they impact stakeholders.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Governance, testing, quality, and reliability language recurs across nearly every responsibility bullet, with no speed-of-shipping phrases present anywhere in the JD.
evidence.domain_risk: Understanding of data privacy, governance, and compliance requirements — with particular sensitivity to regulated healthcare data.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The JD explicitly names regulated healthcare data and compliance requirements as a core skill, an override signal for high domain risk given the platform integrates diagnostics, medication, and doctor consultations for patients.
evidence.collaboration_width: data scientists; analysts; clinical teams; commercial teams; product teams
evidence.data_team_maturity: Experience setting technical standards and delivering high-quality work within a small, high-performing data team.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The JD explicitly describes "a small, high-performing data team" that still needs standards, culture, and mentoring established by this senior hire, and dbt models are named in responsibilities — the function exists and is growing but is not yet at mature scale.
evidence.jd_authorship: Expert-level SQL: window functions, CTEs, advanced aggregations, and cloud warehouse-specific optimisation. Deep proficiency in dbt: models, macros, tests, packages, documentation, exposures, and CI/CD integration.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Requirements and responsibilities name precise technical depth (dbt exposures/macros/CI-CD, BigQuery incremental loading and cost management, LookML, star schemas/SCDs) that only someone who understands the role could write.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Your work will span multiple domains - from commercial performance and marketing effectiveness through to health outcomes analytics and clinical operations
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role explicitly spans commercial, marketing, health-outcomes, and clinical domains with genuinely equal weight across the JD, producing a mixed orientation rather than a single dominant function.
evidence.autonomy_level: We are embarking on a strategic investment in AI-powered analytics, with a full remit to explore and adopt the latest and greatest technologies. You'll be shaping which tools, approaches, and practices Numan adopts as a company.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role sets modelling standards, shapes company-wide AI tooling strategy, and leads end-to-end analytical projects — strategic direction-setting verbs dominate throughout.
evidence.ai_role: Evaluating, trialing, and championing AI-powered analytics tooling - shaping Numan's strategic direction on emerging technologies and what we adopt as a company.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to evaluate and shape AI tooling adoption and build semantic layers/data foundations that AI systems query ("semantic layers, natural language querying"), placing the candidate's output upstream of AI systems — an ai_enabler classification, which also subsumes the ai_user signal present ("practical experience with AI-assisted analytics workflows").
evidence.testing_framing: Acting as a senior technical voice within the analytics engineering function, setting standards for data modelling, testing, documentation, and code quality.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing standards are explicitly something the candidate sets and owns for the whole function, not a tool listed among requirements.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Driving data quality, reliability, and governance across the transformation layer, proactively monitoring consumption and resolving issues before they impact stakeholders.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Governance, compliance, regulated healthcare data sensitivity, and proactive issue resolution before stakeholder impact recur throughout the JD and appear early in the role framing, indicating risk and trust concerns dominate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Designing, building, and maintaining scalable, well-tested dbt data models ... Driving data quality, reliability, and governance across the transformation layer
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both designs new data models across four business domains and shapes new AI tooling strategy (greenfield) while also setting standards and governance across an existing transformation layer (fix_scale), producing a mixed signal.
evidence.work_arrangement: Flexible working options, including a dog-friendly office in Farringdon.
evidence.loss_aversion: Understanding of data privacy, governance, and compliance requirements — with particular sensitivity to regulated healthcare data — explicit regulatory/compliance framing anchors a high risk register given the health platform context.
